_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 2201 batch)
_meta.note: VA LOCAL 5-pack — closing out Stafford BoS (7-of-7 milestone) + advancing Stafford SB (5-of-7): BoS Yeung + BoS Allen + SB Healy + SB Chase + SB Fingerholz. KEY FINDINGS: (1) Yeung — SECOND-term incumbent BoS-Garrisonville (first elected Nov 2021, re-elected Nov 4 2025 vs Bart Randall 57.50%-42.01%, Chair 2023 — first Black BoS Chair in Stafford history). Multi-vector HARD FALSE: bib_marriage q4 multi-cycle (June 2025 + June 2026 Pride proclamation YES both years, Yeung in YES bloc both times) + bib_marriage q2 from 2019 SCPS SOGI nondiscrimination policy YES with dais quote 'There isn't statistically significant evidence to support fears that protecting these groups would lead to un-protecting others. We will protect every child.' + family_child_sov q1 from Aug 2021 SB universal-mask advocacy + maskless-at-McAuliffe-campaign photo (Potomac Local 9/8/2021 'Yeung an advocate for children in facemasks goes maskless at a campaign event') + family_child_sov q2 from 'Reflect Stafford' DEI teacher-recruitment program (campaign 'accomplishments' page). HARD TRUE econ q2 single-cycle April 29 2026 FY27 4¢ tax-rate hike NO vote with dais quote 'I do not support placing this burden on homeowners, seniors, and families on fixed income' + library-hours-reduction concern (FXBG Advance). SOFT signals: pay-raise advocacy for first responders/jail officers (campaign accomplishments + FY26 hearing 'Does that mean jail officers get no pay raise?') = SOFT TRUE pub_just q0; Buc-ee's NIMBY motion-to-deny May 2026 5-2 NO with Guy. (2) Allen — SECOND-term incumbent BoS-Griffis-Widewater (first elected Nov 2019 by 18-vote recount over R Cavalier, re-elected Nov 2023 53.03%-46.47% over I Harper, BoS Vice Chair 2024+2025 under Diggs). HARD FALSE econ q2 MULTI-CYCLE (April 16 2025 MOTIONED the FY26 $0.92 3¢ hike WITH motion-making seat per FXBG Advance + April 29 2026 FY27 $0.9675 4¢ YES) PLUS dais quote 'raising taxes does not equal fiscal irresponsibility … fiscal irresponsibility is not telling the truth and painting a picture.' HARD FALSE bib_marriage q4 MULTI-CYCLE June 2025 + June 2026 Pride proclamations with AFFIRMATIVE ON-DAIS ADVOCACY June 2025 'Board Vice Chair Tinesha Allen and fellow Supervisor Monica Gary implored those in the audience to be more accepting and caring of others' (FFP 2025-06-04). HARD FALSE family_child_sov q2 FOUNDED the post-George-Floyd Stafford multicultural/equity coalition — formal race-categorical civic infrastructure 'food banks were set up nearly three months ago by the coalition formed by Griffis-Widewater Supervisor Tinesha Allen to assist low income, elderly and minority families' (FLS Aug 2020). Sept 21 2021 anti-CRT resolution: ABSENT — NULL not FALSE per cite-only. Religion NULL. Vanderbilt+Shenandoah-Nursing background; Guyana-born first African-American woman on Stafford BoS. (3) Healy — HETERODOX-HIGH-VALUE 25+ year incumbent SB-Rock Hill (first elected Nov 1999 + sworn Jan 2000 — LONGEST-SERVING SB MEMBER IN STAFFORD HISTORY; Chair 7 years incl 2022; ran UNOPPOSED 2023; Georgetown JD attorney + co-partner Leming & Healy P.C.; STAFFORD GOP-ENDORSED + GOP-TEAM-MEMBER listed at staffordgop.com/team-members/patricia-healy-2). PARTY FIX Unknown→R. HARD TRUE bib_marriage q2 + bib_marriage q4 + family_child_sov q1 from Sep 10 2019 4-3 SCPS SOGI nondiscrimination policy NO vote with quoted rationale 'Healy said she does not believe the majority of Stafford residents support the new policies and thinks the board is now exposed to potential litigation because of conflicts that might arise with other policies already on the books.' HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q1 from Feb 10 2022 5-2 mask-mandate-RESCIND vote YES with dais quote 'Since this issue has risen to the top, I feel I have gotten a lot of information, a lot of facts — real facts — that in my opinion show that this is not practicable for our community' (cross-anchor with refuse_state_overreach q2 since Chase/Guy were the NO votes against rescinding). HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 from Feb 25 2026 PRELIMINARY $529.3M FY27 budget SOLE-NO dissent (cleanest partition-of-1 fiscal signal in this entire repo). SOFT TRUE pub_just q0 from 2018 pro-SRO advocacy. NOT a Vlaming amicus signatory (confirmed — only Warner signed from Stafford). Husband Clark Leming is a land-use attorney; 2018 conflict-of-interest dispute about his firm representing developers in Stafford BoS litigation was rebutted by Healy. Religion NULL. (4) Chase — THIRD-term incumbent SB-Falmouth (first elected Nov 2017 over Hirons, re-elected 2021 + Nov 2025 67.14% over Chergosky; VICE CHAIR 2026 — nominated Warner for Chair Jan 13 2026; Georgetown BS Psych + Rice PhD Cognitive Neuropsychology; recently-retired professor; Stafford-Dems-endorsed both 2021+2025; iVoterGuide 'Verified Liberal' with REFUSAL to answer all questionnaire items). PARTY FIX Unknown→D. HARD FALSE bib_marriage q2 + bib_marriage q4 + family_child_sov q2 multi-cell anchor from Sep 11 2019 4-3 SCPS SOGI nondiscrimination policy SHE PERSONALLY CO-SPONSORED with Decatur + Superintendent Kizner with dais quote 'Given the board's failure to respect a transgender student's privacy four years ago, confusion about how to protect a transgender student during an active shooter drill last year and the many negative emails directed at transgender students which I received, I have reached the conclusion that we need to include LGBTQ students in our nondiscrimination policy' + 'There isn't statistically significant evidence to support fears that protecting these groups would lead to un-protecting others. We will protect every child.' HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q1 + q2 THREE-VOTE MASK PATTERN (Jan 20 2022 5-2 KEEP mandate vote YES + seconded the motion 'we took an oath to uphold the law of Virginia' + Feb 10 2022 5-2 RESCIND vote NO with Guy 'I'm concerned that the 22nd is a little too soon' + Feb 22 2022 5-3 staff/bus-driver mask-drop vote NO with Warner+Guy 'asked for the delay for bus drivers'). NOT a Vlaming amicus signatory (confirmed). Religion NULL ('multicultural coalition between local churches' is civic-ecumenical work not personal faith profession). Progressive Voters Guide 2021 listing. (5) Fingerholz — FRESHMAN SB-Hartwood D-FLIP (Nov 4 2025 narrow 50.66%-48.56% / 199-vote-margin win over GOP-backed Steven Epple; previous Hartwood incumbent Alyssa Halstead was Stafford GOP team member who did NOT seek re-election). Stafford-Dems-endorsed + Mountain View PAC of Educators teacher-union endorsed + Maya Guy endorsement displayed on campaign homepage. Finance & Budget Committee CHAIR 2026 (unusually elevated for a freshman, signaling Dem-bloc trust). PARTY FIX Unknown→D. HARD FALSE family_child_sov q2 from 2023 CRRL library-curation defense public-rally quote 'We trust you and the books you have curated for us' (FLS 2023 'community comes out in support of public library' coverage of Stafford couple's puberty/sex-ed book challenge appearing on minor's library mobile app). Sworn Jan 2026 → most Stafford SCPS anchors PREDATE her tenure → NULL not FALSE on Vlaming amicus, Jan/Feb 2022 mask votes, classroom-décor Nov 2022, Sep 21 2021 anti-CRT. Religion 'raised in the church' biographical only → NULL (no denomination/church publicly named). Insurance-license-all-50-states background; 11-yr stay-at-home parent; MOMS Club of Fredericksburg 4-yr president. CROSS-BATCH MILESTONE: STAFFORD BOS NOW 7-OF-7 EVIDENCE-FINALIZED (Vanuch+English+Diggs+Evans+Guy from 2050+2110 batches + Yeung+Allen this batch); STAFFORD SB NOW 5-OF-7 (Warner+Regan from 2110 + Healy+Chase+Fingerholz this batch; remaining: Susan Randall + one slot to verify). PRIDE PROCLAMATION ROLL CALL JUNE 3 2026 NOW FULLY ATTRIBUTED: Diggs SPONSOR+YES + Guy YES + Allen YES + Evans YES + Yeung YES (5 YES bloc) + English SOLE NO + Vanuch absent ill. JUNE 4 2025 PRIDE PROCLAMATION NOW FULLY ATTRIBUTED: Diggs SPONSOR+YES + Allen YES + Gary YES + Yeung YES (4 YES) + English SOLE NO + Bohmke abstain + Vanuch absent. FY27 4-3 TAX VOTE NOW FULLY ATTRIBUTED: YES Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans / NO Yeung+English+Vanuch. STAFFORD SB ORTHODOXY MATRIX EMERGING: Warner+Chase (2019 SOGI sponsors+mask-keeps) = D-aligned anchor; Healy (2019 SOGI dissenter+mask-rescinder+sole-NO fiscal dissent) = R-aligned anchor (Stafford GOP roster); Fingerholz (D-flip freshman) NULL-heavy by tenure; Regan (D first-term Aquia) NULL by candidacy-discipline. RICK-WEST 8TH-CONSECUTIVE-DEFER continuing escalation: rick-west VA Mayor of Chesapeake R level=local TOP of LOCAL priority queue remains slug-collided with rick-west OK State Rep R level=state (refine-records.py line 366 by_slug last-wins). 8th batch to defer (0950+1047+1441+1625 2026-06-02 + 1550+2050+2110+2201 2026-06-04). REQUEST RE-ISSUED 8X: rename VA rick-west to rick-west-chesapeake. Research COMPLETE and stashed since 2026-06-02; would score ~67/100. BLOCKER level persists.

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Garrisonville District

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — campaign site, Ballotpedia profile, and press coverage do not identify a church, denomination, or personal profession of Christian faith.
profile.education: B.S. Business & Information Technology, Howard University; M.S. Science & Technology, Johns Hopkins; Doctorate (Leadership and Organizational Change), Baylor University.
profile.background: Raised in the Netherlands; fluent in Spanish and Dutch. College professor teaching Information Systems and Networking at community colleges; described in 2025 press as science/technology consultant + cybersecurity/IT instructor. Five children — four sons (two UVA grads, one VCU grad working with autistic children in Stafford, one Iraq War veteran) + one daughter (VCU School of Nursing + Duke School of Medicine, former USAF Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist); all five graduated from North Stafford HS. PRIOR OFFICE: Stafford SB Garrisonville Nov 2017 – Dec 2021. FIRST elected to Stafford BoS Nov 2021 (4-yr term 1/2022-12/2025); RE-ELECTED Nov 4 2025 vs Bart Randall 57.50%-42.01% (sworn Jan 2026 for second 4-yr term 1/2026-12/2029). BoS Vice Chair 2022; BoS CHAIR 2023 — first Black BoS Chair in Stafford history. Boards: Central Rappahannock Regional Library System, Fredericksburg Regional Alliance, VRE Operations Board, FAMPO, PRTC, Office of Youth, Rappahannock River Basin Committee, Regional Landfill Board.

pamela-yeung.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Stafford BoS-Garrisonville since Jan 2022, RE-ELECTED Nov 4 2025 (4-yr term 1/2026-12/2029) over Bart Randall 57.50%-42.01%; PRIOR Stafford SB-Garrisonville Nov 2017-Dec 2021. PARTY confirmed D (Stafford Dem Committee endorsed + Foy/Carroll-Foy political mentor; non-partisan ballot label). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null. EDUCATION: Howard BS + Johns Hopkins MS + Baylor doctorate. PROFESSION: college professor (IS/networking, cybersecurity). FAMILY: 5 children all NSHS grads. PRIOR LEADERSHIP: BoS Vice Chair 2022 + Chair 2023 (first Black BoS Chair in Stafford history). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 (LGBTQ PROMOTION) MULTI-CYCLE: June 3 2026 Stafford BoS Pride proclamation 5-1 YES bloc (Yeung among Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans+Yeung 5 YES; English sole NO; Vanuch absent ill); June 3 2025 BoS Pride proclamation 4-1-1 YES (Yeung among Diggs+Gary+Allen+Yeung 4 YES; English sole NO; Bohmke abstain; Vanuch absent). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2 (GENDER IDEOLOGY): As Stafford SB member Sep 2019 voted YES on 4-3 SOGI nondiscrimination policy adding 'sexual orientation' and 'gender identity' to protected categories with dais quote 'There isn't statistically significant evidence to support fears that protecting these groups would lead to un-protecting others. We will protect every child' — affirmative on-dais defense of categorical gender-identity protection. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q1 (PARENTAL SOVEREIGNTY ON COVID): As SB member Aug 2021 was LEAD ADVOCATE for mandatory universal masking of all children entering Stafford schools ('Yeung did not support a motion to make masking optional because she wanted to see universal masking, citing that Stafford had high community transmission and less than 50 percent vaccinated') COMPOUNDED by Sep 8 2021 Potomac Local 'Yeung an advocate for children in facemasks goes maskless at a campaign event' photographing her maskless at McAuliffe campaign event during same period — public hypocrisy on the children-masking advocacy. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2 (DEI/EQUITY IN K-12): Founded the 'Reflect Stafford' initiative explicitly framed by her campaign as recruiting/retaining 'teachers of diverse backgrounds to better reflect student body demographics' (yeungforstafford.com/accomplishments) + as SB member 'spurred changes in policies and introduction of new policies to meet the needs of the increasingly diverse student body, including increasing the number of diverse teachers, social workers and counselors' (jennifercarrollfoy.com tribute) — multi-vector race-categorical equity-framework advocacy in K-12. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 (FISCAL/BUDGET RESTRAINT): April 29 2026 BoS FY27 budget — Yeung one of three NO votes against 4¢ real-estate tax-rate hike (FY26 $0.92 → FY27 $0.9675) with dais quote 'I do not support placing this burden on homeowners, seniors, and families on fixed income' and flagged that 'cuts to library funding have resulted in a reduction of branch operating hours' (FXBG Advance). Single-cycle restraint signal (FY26 prior year she was in YES coalition) so HARD on the anchored cell but not a multi-cycle pattern. SOFT TRUE public_justice q0 (BACK THE BLUE): Multiple instances of public advocacy for first-responder/jail-officer pay raises — at FY26 budget hearing pressed 'Does that mean jail officers get no pay raise?' + campaign accomplishments lists 'secure pay raises for teachers, bus drivers and first responders' + as SB voted to place armed protection officers at Hartwood/Moncure elementary schools (though 'expressed concerns'). SOFT NOT HARD — pay-raise advocacy is institutional-budget activity, not Rom. 13:4-affirmative back-the-blue Mehaffey-style. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (no abortion/conception/PRC statement), biblical_marriage q0 (no marriage-definition statement), family_child_sov q0 (no school-choice statement; all-public-school advocacy not framed anti-choice), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (no religion disclosed; no invocation/Christmas/RFRA position), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (no statement), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no bail/charging/Rom-13:4 statement; Mehaffey 2A-defiance-bloc absence May 2026 = NULL not FALSE per cite-only), refuse_state_overreach q0/q2/q4 (no adult/employer COVID/RGGI/ESG/nullification statement; school mask record already cited under family_child_sov), refuse_state_overreach q1 (single soft signal on ICE warehouse 'Immigration policy is federal, but its impacts are local' = SOFT FALSE direction but kept as NULL given thin direct-cite), refuse_state_overreach q3 (data-center 750-ft setback abstained 10/22/2025 = SOFT signal but ABSTENTION = NULL not TRUE per cite-only), border_immigration q1/q2 (Sheriff Decatur sets 287(g); Yeung silent on detainer policy), self_defense q0/q1 (no statement; Spanberger 2A package May 2026 silence = NULL). Net 4 HARD FALSE + 1 HARD TRUE + 1 SOFT TRUE on cited cells; D-aligned progressive with single-cycle FY27 fiscal-restraint reversal. Buc-ee's NIMBY May 2026 NO motion-to-deny (5-2 with Guy) = district-fiscal-impact concern, not categorical anti-growth → not separately scored. Recommend revisiting after next FY28 budget vote + sanctuary/2A position emergence + any further DEI school-board action.

2.note: HARD: As Stafford SB member Sep 2019 voted YES on 4-3 SOGI nondiscrimination policy adding 'sexual orientation' and 'gender identity' to protected categories. Dais quote: 'There isn’t statistically significant evidence to support fears that protecting these groups would lead to un-protecting others. We will protect every child' — affirmative on-dais defense of categorical gender-identity protection in K-12.

4.note: HARD multi-cycle: June 3 2026 Stafford BoS Pride proclamation 5-1 YES (English sole NO, Vanuch absent ill) — Yeung among Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans+Yeung 5 YES by mathematical deduction; June 3 2025 4-1-1 — Yeung among Diggs+Gary+Allen+Yeung 4 YES.

1.note: HARD: As SB member Aug 2021 was lead advocate for mandatory universal masking of all children entering Stafford schools — 'Yeung did not support a motion to make masking optional because she wanted to see universal masking, citing that Stafford had high community transmission and less than 50 percent vaccinated.' COMPOUNDED by Sept 8 2021 Potomac Local headline 'Yeung an advocate for children in facemasks goes maskless at a campaign event' — masked-children advocacy with maskless-adult campaign event behavior. Opposite of parental sovereignty over child medical/mental-health interventions.

2.note: HARD multi-vector: Founded 'Reflect Stafford' initiative explicitly framed as recruiting/retaining 'teachers of diverse backgrounds to better reflect student body demographics' (campaign accomplishments page) + as SB member 'spurred changes in policies and introduction of new policies to meet the needs of the increasingly diverse student body, including increasing the number of diverse teachers, social workers and counselors' (Foy mentor tribute). Race-categorical equity-framework advocacy in K-12 personnel + curriculum direction.

HARD single-cycle: April 29 2026 Stafford BoS FY27 budget — Yeung one of three NO votes (Yeung+English+Vanuch) against 4¢ tax-rate hike (FY26 $0.92 → FY27 $0.9675) with dais quote 'I do not support placing this burden on homeowners, seniors, and families on fixed income' + flagged 'cuts to library funding have resulted in a reduction of branch operating hours.' HARD on the anchored cell; single-cycle restraint signal (FY26 was YES) so not a multi-cycle pattern.

0.note: SOFT: Pay-raise advocacy for first responders/jail officers — at FY26 budget hearing pressed 'Does that mean jail officers get no pay raise?' + campaign accomplishments lists 'secure pay raises for teachers, bus drivers and first responders' as top accomplishment. SOFT not HARD because institutional-budget pay advocacy is not Rom. 13:4-affirmative Mehaffey-style back-the-blue.

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Griffis-Widewater District

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — campaign Facebook, news bio profiles, VPAP, and county bio do not identify a church, denomination, or personal profession of Christian faith.
profile.education: International Baccalaureate (HS) → Posse Foundation Full Tuition Leadership Scholarship to Vanderbilt University (B.A. Political Science) → Shenandoah University (Nursing degree).
profile.background: Born Georgetown, Guyana; immigrated to Brooklyn NY in elementary school; Stafford resident since 2015. Nurse by profession; also serves as Vanderbilt University Corps interviewer for students in Stafford and surrounding areas. Husband is an elementary math/science teacher; elementary-school-age daughter. FIRST African-American woman elected to Stafford BoS — defeated long-serving Republican Jack Cavalier in Nov 2019 by 18 votes after recount (took office Jan 2020); RE-ELECTED Nov 2023 vs Ind. Jennifer Harper 53.03%-46.47% (4-yr term 1/2024-12/2027 — STILL IN FIRST RE-ELECTION TERM, did not run Nov 2025). BoS VICE CHAIR 2024 + 2025 under Chair Diggs; Maya Guy now Vice Chair 2026. Founded post-George-Floyd Stafford multicultural/equity coalition (2020). Current committees: Joint Schools Working Committee; Local Finance Board (OPEB); Military Installation Resilience Review Policy Commission.

tinesha-allen.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Stafford BoS-Griffis-Widewater since Jan 2020 (NOT a 2026 freshman — second term, re-elected Nov 2023 53.03%-46.47% over Ind. Jennifer Harper; 4-yr term 1/2024-12/2027); BoS Vice Chair 2024+2025 under Chair Diggs. PARTY confirmed D (Stafford Dem-aligned; non-partisan ballot label). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null. EDUCATION: Vanderbilt BA Poli Sci + Shenandoah Nursing. PROFESSION: nurse + Vanderbilt Posse Corps interviewer. FIRST AFRICAN-AMERICAN WOMAN ON STAFFORD BOS (defeated incumbent R Jack Cavalier 2019 by 18-vote recount margin). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 (FISCAL RESTRAINT) MULTI-CYCLE: (1) April 16 2025 PERSONALLY MOTIONED the FY26 $0.92 3¢ tax-rate hike from $0.89 ('Vice Chair Tinesha Allen … made the motion to approve the $0.92 tax rate') — joined YES by Diggs+Gary+Yeung; NO by Vanuch+English+Bohmke; (2) April 29 2026 voted YES on FY27 $0.9675 4¢ hike (4-3 with Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans YES vs Yeung+English+Vanuch NO); (3) dais quote refuting fiscal-restraint framing: 'raising taxes does not equal fiscal irresponsibility … fiscal irresponsibility is not telling the truth and painting a picture' (FXBG Advance editorial). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 (LGBTQ PROMOTION) MULTI-CYCLE: June 3 2026 BoS Pride proclamation 5-1 YES (English sole NO; Vanuch absent ill; Allen among 5 YES); June 3 2025 BoS Pride proclamation 4-1-1 YES with AFFIRMATIVE ON-DAIS ADVOCACY — 'Board Vice Chair Tinesha Allen and fellow Supervisor Monica Gary implored those in the audience to be more accepting and caring of others' (FFP 6/4/2025). Multi-cycle + on-dais advocacy = HARD. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2 (DEI/EQUITY): Founded post-George-Floyd 2020 Stafford multicultural/equity coalition — formal civic-government infrastructure framed around race/equity categories: 'food banks were set up nearly three months ago by the coalition formed by Griffis-Widewater Supervisor Tinesha Allen to assist low income, elderly and minority families … also provides assistance to gain access to public health, voter registration and employment preparation training' (FLS Aug 2020); 2025 actively defended equity framework even while criticizing slow results — 'this is a waste of time to people because we're not having anything enforceable, anything meaningful' (FLS — criticism is OF lack of teeth, not OF the framework). Sept 21 2021 anti-CRT/pronoun resolution: ABSENT for the vote — NULL not TRUE/FALSE per cite-only discipline (absence on a values-aligned vote ≠ opposition but ≠ position either). NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (no abortion/PRC/Dobbs statement), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or trans-ideology specific vote located), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1 (no school-choice or parental-notification statement), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (religion not disclosed; no invocation/Christmas/RFRA/Mark-Taylor statement), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (no statement), public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (no back-the-blue affirmative advocacy located; voting YES on routine sheriff budget items does NOT clear public_justice q0 high-bar per rubric), refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (no Spanberger 2A response May 2026 = NULL per cite-only; Hartwood solar farm motion-to-approve April 2025 = soft directional alignment with state energy mandates but thin single-vote → NULL not FALSE; data-center setback NO votes Oct/Dec 2025 cut AGAINST neighborhood-subsidiarity but are pro-development not pro-state-mandate → NULL), border_immigration q1/q2 (Sheriff Decatur sets 287(g); Allen silent on detainer policy = NULL), self_defense q0/q1 (12/17/2019 Stafford 2A Sanctuary predates her tenure by ~2 weeks → null; no personal 2A statement). Net 3 HARD FALSE on cited cells; D-aligned progressive incumbent with multi-vector fiscal+social+DEI alignment. Recommend revisiting after FY28 budget vote + first abortion/sanctuary/Sheriff-budget vote + any post-Spanberger-2A-package statement.

4.note: HARD multi-cycle + AFFIRMATIVE ADVOCACY: June 3 2026 BoS Pride proclamation 5-1 YES (Allen among Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans+Yeung 5 YES); June 3 2025 4-1-1 YES with on-dais advocacy 'Board Vice Chair Tinesha Allen and fellow Supervisor Monica Gary implored those in the audience to be more accepting and caring of others' — not silent assent.

2.note: HARD: Founded post-George-Floyd 2020 Stafford multicultural/equity coalition — race-categorical civic-government infrastructure 'to assist low income, elderly and minority families … gain access to public health, voter registration and employment preparation training.' 2025 defended the equity framework even while criticizing slow enforcement ('this is a waste of time … because we're not having anything enforceable, anything meaningful') — DEI/equity framing, not values-neutral civic mutual aid.

HARD multi-cycle: April 16 2025 PERSONALLY MOTIONED the FY26 $0.92 3¢ tax-rate hike from $0.89 ('Vice Chair Tinesha Allen … made the motion to approve the $0.92 tax rate'); April 29 2026 YES on FY27 $0.9675 4¢ hike (4-3 with Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans YES vs Yeung+English+Vanuch NO). DAIS QUOTE refuting fiscal-restraint framing: 'raising taxes does not equal fiscal irresponsibility … fiscal irresponsibility is not telling the truth and painting a picture.'

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — no church/parish/denomination publicly identified in any campaign, official, or press source.
profile.education: B.A. Spanish Literature, University of Maryland Baltimore County (UMBC); J.D. Georgetown University Law Center.
profile.background: Attorney in private practice in Stafford County since 1986; co-partner at Leming & Healy P.C. (North Stafford) for 30+ years. Husband Clark Leming (land-use attorney, partner at same firm); one daughter Sara Leming (Mountain View HS grad — cited reason Healy ran for SB in 1999: 'If it hadn’t been for my daughter, I would not be on the school board'). Stafford resident since 1985. FIRST ELECTED Stafford SB-Rock Hill November 1999 (sworn Jan 1 2000); LONGEST-SERVING SB MEMBER IN STAFFORD HISTORY (records dating to 1925) — re-elected every cycle since 1999; ran UNOPPOSED Nov 2023; current 4-yr term 1/2024-12/2027. Leadership history: SB Chair 7 years (including 2022); SB Vice-Chair multiple terms including 2023; served alongside 33 different board members. STAFFORD GOP-ENDORSED and listed on staffordgop.com 'Team Members' page (R-aligned despite non-partisan VA SB ballot label).

patricia-healy.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Stafford SB-Rock Hill since Jan 2000 (LONGEST-SERVING SB MEMBER IN STAFFORD HISTORY, records to 1925); current 4-yr term 1/2024-12/2027 after running UNOPPOSED Nov 2023; SB Chair 7 years (incl 2022) + Vice-Chair multiple terms incl 2023. PARTY FIX: Unknown→R (Stafford GOP-endorsed candidate + Stafford GOP 'Team Members' page listing at staffordgop.com/team-members/patricia-healy-2). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null. EDUCATION: UMBC BA Spanish Lit + Georgetown JD. PROFESSION: attorney since 1986; co-partner Leming & Healy P.C. with husband Clark Leming. FAMILY: husband Clark + daughter Sara (Mountain View HS). HARD TRUE biblical_marriage q2 (GENDER IDEOLOGY DIRECTION): Sep 10 2019 cast NO vote (one of three NOs: Healy + Hollerback + Hazard) on 4-3 SCPS SOGI nondiscrimination policy that added 'sexual orientation' and 'gender identity' to protected categories — direction: opposed categorical gender-identity protection. HARD TRUE biblical_marriage q4 (LGBTQ PROMOTION IN SCHOOLS): same 2019 SOGI NO vote — direction: opposed expansion of LGBTQ-protected categorical framework in K-12 policy. HARD TRUE family_child_sovereignty q1 (PARENTAL SOVEREIGNTY ON GENDER): same 2019 SOGI NO vote with quoted representative-government rationale 'Healy said she does not believe the majority of Stafford residents support the new policies and thinks the board is now exposed to potential litigation because of conflicts that might arise with other policies already on the books' — opposed displacing parental/community policy authority via SOGI compelled-protection framework. HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q1 (DECLINED STATE STRINGS): Feb 10 2022 voted YES on 5-2 rescinding Stafford SCPS universal mask mandate (against Chase+Guy NO votes; cross-anchor with Chase's HARD FALSE on the same vote) with on-dais quote 'Since this issue has risen to the top, I feel I have gotten a lot of information, a lot of facts — real facts — that in my opinion show that this is not practicable for our community.' Direction: declined state COVID-mandate alignment that compelled local policy continuation. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 (FISCAL/BUDGET RESTRAINT): Feb 25 2026 cast SOLE NO dissent on PRELIMINARY $529.3M FY27 SCPS budget (board passed 6-1) citing rising worker insurance costs and budget-cost trajectory — cleanest partition-of-1 fiscal-restraint signal located across all Stafford SB members. (Joined unanimous 4/29/2026 final $525.9M adoption — final was unanimous so weak signal; the Feb 25 prelim NO is the anchor.) SOFT TRUE public_justice q0 (BACK THE BLUE / SRO): 2018 on-record advocacy for additional SROs and recruitment/training timeline (Stafford currently has 13 SROs). SOFT because pre-current-cycle and pro-SRO-funding ≠ Mehaffey-style affirmative back-the-blue.
